item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We should get Djkaktus on it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This just seems silly to me. There is way too little scientific analysis in there for my taste. How did he come to the attention of the Foundation, what's his back story, why can't you determine the reason for his growth, etc. If this had a -J I would not mind it, but I just can't take this seriously.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Considering the Heritage Collection has been discontinued and archived, I don't think any new additions will be made any time soon. Even if it were still up and running, though, this SCP in particular didn't contribute much to the site's history and more so to its popularity, when considering its exposure from the Confinement series.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Does being on the thumbnail of the first Confinement episode qualify this one for the Heritage Collection?

Cause I mean, on one hand, one can't qualify every skip for the Heritage collection because it was referenced by Confinement (otherwise it would be a pretty long list), but on the other hand, this meets all the other requirements and now the first thing I think of about SCP-082 is badminton.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait, 082 is Dr. Bright? We need to know more about this claim, man!

Also, I know it's juvenile, but i'm glad that I am the 69th comment on this page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: literally all i knew about this dude before reading this was "we're all too busy for badminton". glad i read more about it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i feel like the clinical tone is flowery at times, and I think that the idea is good, but it is not at its best.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really enjoyed this. Good writing with a fine tenor. The whole set up of the final 'joke' was very well done. Enjoyed the details.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm assuming this cannibal has a case of schizophrenia but I'm not a doctor.
+1 for the good writing and strange anomaly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: meh

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Who the fuck spoke to this guy of Dr. Bright?

What a stupid question, he probably went for a dinner with Fernand.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He's roughly the size of a b-a-a-a-a-rge…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fernand is an interesting Series one SCP, though I don't really feel like we get to know him all that well. The description of its appearance is great, and it mannerisms are bizarre and interesting. But maybe an expansion on how he came into Foundation custody or maybe some interview logs would help flesh him out more? I'm surprised they just left all that metal inside him, maybe carbondating some of those knives or blades could help with identifying how old he is? Just some food for thought. ^_^

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What if he is a version of dr. Jack bright from a universe where he was put through SCP-914 on very fine? (Please note this is not serious)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would love to see this guy to up against Able, just for the sheer lols.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Confinement brought me here to rediscover and… Man… I think it's one of the first SCP I've ever read. It gives some kind of nostalgia.

Not very interesting, not very elaborate, not very funny (I forgot that lame, cheesy pun) but, natural. A simple monster.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't think that's how it works. He seems to not be able to control himself, so making him peaceful would just make him be the same but with a calm personality.

Plus he once believed he was Big Bird, how friendlier can you get?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If 082 reacts to media, why haven't a edited video of something to tone down the violence within him, make up peace and zen bullshit or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Voct beat me to it — as soon as I finished reading about Fernand I realized, "this is a real Giant, from the fairy tales—but with real people quirks!" Very cool old school SCP in my estimation, and he gets my plus one.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: After the evidence found here the images was decided not to be complient with the site license and thus removed, with permission from administrator Dexanote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Should the pic be taken down, then?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: so someone on reddit just found the source of the image and it's a fucking Honeycombs commercial, this is so surreal to me like what a world we live in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That last line is so cheesy, but a good kind of cheesy! It had me grinning ear to ear.

this is such an interesting character you've created, the nigh-incomprehensible nature of his personality is so well done! +1

item.title: Re: Ummm...
item.content:encoded: to be fair, a lot of the assertions that he was relatively mundane pre-dated my identification of him as being literally a character from a fairy tale.

item.title: Re: Ummm...
item.content:encoded: I am continuously perplexed by the recurring assertion that this isn't anomalous. Fernand is an immortal fairy tale giant. How is that in any way mundane?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: the fact that it has blades and bullets logded in its flesh and its, yanno, still alive, dont oyu think that would be the tip off?

item.title: Staff Post - Closed
item.content:encoded: If you don't have anything to contribute to discussion other than "^", then it's probably best not to post at all. Food for thought next time.

item.title: Re: Ummm...
item.content:encoded: See MacLeod's comment above.

item.title: Ummm...
item.content:encoded: Not sure why this is a SCP, it's not anomalous in any way and why would the foundation keep it? Still a great one, UPVOTE!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He's resilient to a level that literally no human can reach. And he kinda breaks the Square-Cube Law (but only kinda). And he doesn't seem to age. All pretty solid prerequisites for classifying him as anomalous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So they have him locked up because he's just a large human full of melee weapons and he can't understand fiction? Am I missing something?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: you could probably get a psychologist to write entire books about Fernand and still not know what the fuck is going on in his head. and that's why I love this one +1

item.title: Reminded me of something
item.content:encoded: Sie sind das Essen und wir sind die Jäger!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Author here

I love how people hate the last line. Probably because its hammy and not funny. But that's why it perfectly suits Fernand. It's not apparent that he has any concept of humor or puns. Imagine him saying things like this all the time and your in his presence, right in front of him.

Do you laugh, do you take him seriously. He's a giant, friendly cannibal. This isn't a kid's movie. He isn't a misunderstood monster. He is a monster, and he's beyond reason or understanding.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Author here

You nailed! Right on the head! You described Fernand better than I could have myself and probably had better understanding of him than I did when I created him.

Fernand is an orphan from the worlds of Tolkein, the Brothers Grimm and other fairy tales. He lies somewhere between monster and your weird, quirky uncle. He's both an ogre of a man and a man of an ogre. Someone both fun and horrifying, someone you'd want to know about but not meet in person.

I've been thinking of adding more to the article but I worry that my imagination wouldn't be as fun as the readers, who could fill in all the blanks with ideas that would make them happy. That's why I love skips. They spark the imagination, but its the reader's brain that sets the mind on fire.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The article is good, brilliant, kind of unique in its own.

(Specially because it's a Humanoid SCP that is not overly Intelligent, Powerful or Perfect like the others.)

+1

You go Roi Fernand.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Don't forget Brian Cox.
I like this SCP. The physical attributes alone wouldn't have interested me much, but this psychological makeup really drew me in. Upvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fernand is an ogre. Literally, a fairytale ogre. This is why he has trouble telling fact from fiction: he's a fictional character come to life. It's also why he has "heightened intelligence in the form of memory and puzzle-solving": this is exactly what an ogre in a story would have, remembering inconvenient facts and solving your cleverest attempts to stymie him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Showing its age in the worst possible way. What really killed it for me was the painfully lame pun at the end.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Presumably he'd imagine that changing appearance like that is normal for people.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So wait, if he can't understand fiction and thinks its all real, how did he react to Hannibal Lecter going from Anthony Hopkins to Gaspard Ulliel to Mads Mikkelsen?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am imagining a giant with a crown and Kingseeker Frampt's teeth.

… Hold on, lemme draw that real quick

EDIT: Job done. http://pbs.twimg.com/media/Bz4rIaAIEAA-GJh.jpg

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My personal headcanon has now this SCP being the actual SCP-universe counterpart of Obélix.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The physical description, fluency in French, and musical taste immediately made me think he really is Adam (Frankenstein's creature), albeit with a different fate, or just lost memory from his suicide attempt. But that's why I like it so much!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has a huge amount of potential but really needs a rewrite.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's not exceptionally anomalous, but its physiology is something that simply does not occur in humans. That hair is almost ten times as thick as that of a normal human. Its muscles are so thick that they block x-rays. Its fist is wider than your forearm is long. And somehow it's genetically human.

… is it just me or does this motherfucker sound a lot like a stupid, cannibal version of Wreck-It Ralph?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When it speaks, it does so through enormous, clenched teeth.

Yup. I don't see anything interesting in this article, or even anything particularly anomalous. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dr Bright? WTF

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Let's say hello to our friendly neighborhood cannibal with delusions of grandeur! 1+ enthusiastically upvoted! (careful, you are tasty)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, it's a polite wendigo. A gentleman with taste and urges, and both are "human heads".
Ok, I find it charming, maybe too much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this is a good example of scps creepy pasta roots; the Hannibal references are a bit much and the prose is more flowery than nowadays nowadays but that's part of why I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't belive i hadn't upvoted this!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I didn't feel like it was charming. Honestly, the entire piece just came off as fucking stupid; the only salvageable aspect, in my mind, is the idea of a man who just happens to be, like, magnified. Not just growing tall and big, but every part of him is bigger.

And yes, I do realize that I'm using the phrase "fucking stupid" relatively frequently, but I figure it applies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh…. I really feel like I shouldn't like this, since it isn't the most well-written SCP by any means, but I just found it to be very charming. The list of all the people he's claimed to be amused me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just noticed something:

When questioned about these lies he gives the excuse,

According to SCP-082, he is a

I don't think we refer to SCP's as "he".

Also, I remember reading this and thinking it was funny, but when I look at it now I'm not really interested by it. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Last sentence did it for me .
Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: me, as well. Especially Dog.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kinda reminds me of a Super Mutant from the Fallout series.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You won my vote at "Obleix (Sidekick of Asterix.)"
